name: Cartoon
suffix: cute cartoon style, big expressive eyes, clean bold lines, cheerful bright colors
description: Bright, bouncy drawings like your favorite TV shows! Cartoons keep shapes simple and colors cheerful so every character bursts with personality.
fact: Cartoons squash and stretch characters to make their movement feel bouncy and alive!

name: Watercolor
suffix: watercolor painting, soft washes of color, wet-on-wet technique, gentle dreamy tones
description: Painting with colors mixed into water, so they flow and melt into each other. Watercolor pictures look soft, dreamy, and a little bit magical.
fact: Watercolor paint is mostly water, so the colors flow and blend into each other like magic!

name: Pixel Art
suffix: pixel art, 16-bit retro video game style, crisp pixels, vibrant palette
description: Pictures built from tiny colored squares called pixels, just like old video games. Zoom in and you can count every single dot!
fact: Pixel art is made of tiny squares! Old video games used it because computers could only draw a few dots at a time.

name: Van Gogh
suffix: in the style of Vincent van Gogh, thick swirling brushstrokes, impasto oil painting, starry night colors
description: Painting like Vincent van Gogh, a famous Dutch artist who used thick, swirly strokes of oil paint and glowing blues and yellows.
fact: Van Gogh painted with paint so thick you can still see his brushstrokes bumping up off the canvas!

name: Comic Book
suffix: comic book style, bold ink outlines, halftone dots, dynamic action colors
description: The style of superhero comics: bold black outlines, bright punchy colors, and lots of action. KAPOW!
fact: Comics use tiny dots of color called halftones — look really close at an old comic and you'll see them!

name: Claymation
suffix: claymation style, plasticine clay model, stop motion animation look, soft studio lighting
description: Everything looks like it was squished and sculpted out of soft modeling clay, just like the characters in stop-motion movies.
fact: Claymation movies move clay models a tiny bit, snap a photo, and repeat — about 24 photos for just one second of film!

name: Origami
suffix: origami style, folded paper art, clean crisp paper folds, minimalist
description: The Japanese art of paper folding! Everything looks like it was carefully folded from crisp, clean sheets of paper.
fact: Origami is the Japanese art of paper folding — 'ori' means fold and 'kami' means paper!

name: Stained Glass
suffix: stained glass window style, bold black outlines, glowing jewel-toned colors
description: Pictures made from pieces of colorful glass held together by dark lines, glowing like a window with the sun shining through.
fact: Long ago, stained glass windows told stories with pictures for people who couldn't read!

name: Crayon
suffix: crayon drawing, waxy texture, childlike drawing style, bright colors on white paper
description: Cheerful, waxy drawings like the ones you make with a brand-new box of crayons — bright colors and happy scribbles.
fact: The biggest crayon ever made weighed as much as a car — and it was blue!

name: Low Poly
suffix: low poly 3D render, flat shaded triangles, geometric shapes, pastel colors
description: 3D art built entirely from flat triangles, like a paper sculpture inside a computer. Simple shapes, but so cool!
fact: Low poly art builds whole pictures out of triangles, just like the very first 3D video games!

name: Great Wave
suffix: ukiyo-e Japanese woodblock print style, in the style of Hokusai, flat colors, elegant wave patterns
description: Japanese woodblock printing, like the famous artist Hokusai. Flat colors, elegant lines, and beautiful curling waves.
fact: Ukiyo-e pictures were carved into wood, covered in ink, and pressed onto paper. Hokusai's Great Wave is the most famous one ever!

name: Pop Art
suffix: pop art style, bold flat colors, repeated patterns, screen print look, Andy Warhol inspired
description: Loud, fun art from the 1960s with super-bold colors and repeating patterns. It turns everyday things into eye-popping art!
fact: Pop artists like Andy Warhol turned everyday things — even soup cans — into famous art!

name: Impressionist
suffix: impressionist oil painting, in the style of Claude Monet, soft dappled brushstrokes, shimmering light and color
description: Painting light itself! Quick, soft dabs of color that shimmer like sunshine on water, made famous by Claude Monet.
fact: Impressionists painted outdoors really fast to catch the light before it changed — at first people thought their paintings looked unfinished!

name: Pointillism
suffix: pointillism painting, in the style of Georges Seurat, made entirely of thousands of tiny colorful dots
description: Whole pictures made of nothing but tiny dots of color. Stand back and the dots magically blend together!
fact: Georges Seurat spent two whole years painting one picture out of millions of tiny dots!

name: Cubism
suffix: cubist painting in the style of Pablo Picasso, geometric shapes, fractured angles, bold flat colors
description: Pictures built from triangles, squares and funny angles, showing things from lots of directions at once — like a puzzle of a face.
fact: Picasso painted faces from the front AND the side at the same time — that's why cubist noses look sideways!

name: Surrealism
suffix: surrealist dreamlike painting, whimsical impossible scene, floating objects, gentle dream colors, Salvador Dali inspired
description: Dream pictures! Things float, melt, and mix together in ways that could never happen in real life.
fact: Surrealist painters made pictures of dreams — Salvador Dalí painted melting clocks and elephants on stilt legs!

name: Paper Collage
suffix: paper collage art, cut paper shapes in the style of Henri Matisse, layered colorful construction paper, crisp cut edges
description: Pictures made by cutting and layering colorful paper shapes — snip, arrange, and glue!
fact: When the painter Henri Matisse got older, he 'painted with scissors' — cutting big shapes out of colored paper!

name: Mosaic
suffix: ancient mosaic artwork, made of small colorful ceramic tiles, visible grout lines, Roman mosaic style
description: Pictures made from hundreds of little colored tiles, like the ancient Romans used to decorate their floors and walls.
fact: Some Roman mosaic floors are 2,000 years old and their tiny tiles are still bright and colorful today!

name: Egyptian
suffix: ancient Egyptian wall painting style, hieroglyphic mural, figures in side profile, gold and ochre tones, papyrus texture
description: How the ancient Egyptians painted on temple walls: people standing sideways, bold outlines, and glowing desert-gold colors.
fact: Egyptians drew people with sideways faces but front-facing shoulders — and they kept that style for 3,000 years!

name: Ink Wash
suffix: traditional Chinese ink wash painting, sumi-e brush strokes, flowing black ink with soft color accents, rice paper texture
description: Gentle ink brushed onto paper with soft, flowing strokes — the beautiful traditional painting of China and Japan.
fact: Ink wash painters practice single brushstrokes for years — one perfect stroke can be a whole bamboo leaf!

name: Anime
suffix: anime style, cel shaded animation, expressive sparkly eyes, clean line art, vibrant colors
description: The famous Japanese cartoon style with big expressive eyes, clean lines, and bright colors.
fact: Anime artists draw about 24 pictures for every single second of a cartoon!

name: Kawaii
suffix: kawaii style, super cute Japanese sticker art, chibi, pastel colors, adorable round shapes, happy faces
description: The Japanese art of super-cute! Round shapes, happy faces, and soft pastel colors that make everything adorable.
fact: 'Kawaii' means 'cute' in Japanese — in Japan even police stations and airplanes have cute mascots!

name: Street Art
suffix: vibrant street art mural, bold graffiti style painting, spray paint texture, dynamic energetic shapes, colorful urban wall art
description: Giant, colorful murals like the ones painted on city walls — bold, energetic, and impossible to miss.
fact: Some street artists paint pictures on buildings taller than 20 houses, using hundreds of cans of spray paint!

name: Neon Glow
suffix: neon glow art, bright glowing neon outlines on a dark background, luminous synthwave colors, electric light painting
description: Bright glowing lines that shine out of the dark like electric rainbow lights.
fact: Neon signs glow because electricity makes the gas inside glass tubes light up like magic!
